changes_made: Added BroadcastReceiver, Intent, and Log imports
Created companion object with scanner action and intent extra key constants
Implemented scanReceiver as a BroadcastReceiver object handling 'nlscan.action.SCANNER_RESULT' broadcasts
Added barcode data extraction and validation logic
Created ScanResult object construction from extracted data
Added debug/warning logging for scan events

patterns_discovered.description: Standard Android pattern for listening to system/app broadcasts with action filtering
Centralized definition of action and intent extra keys following Android SDK conventions
isNullOrBlank() validation for critical barcode data before processing
Using getStringExtra() with fallback to UNKNOWN for optional barcode type
TAG-based logging with appropriate severity levels (Log.w, Log.d)

gotchas_discovered.description: Debouncing and SharedFlow emission are deferred to subsequent subtasks, leaving the receiver incomplete for production use
The code instantiates ScanResult but the class definition is not shown in diff, assumes it exists elsewhere
BroadcastReceiver is created but no code shown for registerReceiver/unregisterReceiver lifecycle binding
Context parameter in onReceive() can be null, but code uses it directly without null check (though not used in current implementation)
Intent can be null in onReceive(), guarded by action check but potential edge case

approach_outcome.description: BroadcastReceiver successfully implemented for Newland MT90 scanner integration
approach_outcome.completeness: Partial - core receiver logic complete, debouncing and reactive emission pending
approach_outcome.quality_assessment: Well-structured with proper null checks, logging, and Newland SDK compliance

recommendations.recommendation: Implement receiver lifecycle management by registering/unregistering in Fragment/Activity onStart/onStop or use ViewModel-aware approach
Implement debouncing mechanism to prevent duplicate scan processing from rapid successive scans
Implement SharedFlow/StateFlow emission to propagate scan results to observers/collectors
Consider using LocalBroadcastManager or modern WorkManager alternatives instead of system broadcasts for better performance
Add try-catch around intent extra extraction to handle unexpected data types gracefully
Create unit tests using RobolectricBroadcastReceiver or mock framework to validate scan data extraction
